Class SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.MechanicalDesign
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.filter.FilterMechanicalDesign
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.filter.SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign extends FilterMechanicalDesign
Mechanical design for sulfur filter vessels per ASME VIII Div 1.

Extends the general FilterMechanicalDesign with sulfur-specific behaviour: reading gas flow rate and element count from SulfurFilter, providing sulfur removal data in the JSON report, and calculating maintenance intervals based on solid S8 loading rates.

Usage: 


SulfurFilter filter = new SulfurFilter("S8 Filter", stream);
filter.run();
filter.initMechanicalDesign();
SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign design =
    (SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign) filter.getMechanicalDesign();
design.setMaxOperationPressure(20.0);
design.setMaxOperationTemperature(273.15 + 50.0);
design.calcDesign();
System.out.println(design.toJson());
Version:
1.1
Author:
esol
See Also:

      Serialization version UID.

  • Constructor Details

    • SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign

      public SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign(ProcessEquipmentInterface equipment)
      Creates a new SulfurFilterMechanicalDesign.
      Parameters:
      equipment - the SulfurFilter equipment to design

  • Method Details

    • getGasFlowRateKgHr

      protected double getGasFlowRateKgHr()
      Returns the gas mass flow rate in kg/hr for element sizing.

      Default implementation reads from the inlet stream. Subclasses may override to use a filter-type-specific method (e.g., a separate tracked flow rate field).

      Overrides:
      getGasFlowRateKgHr in class FilterMechanicalDesign
      Returns:
      gas flow rate in kg/hr

    • getInitialElementCount

      protected int getInitialElementCount()
      Returns the initial number of elements from the equipment before sizing.

      Default returns 1. Subclasses may override to read from the specific filter equipment.

      Overrides:
      getInitialElementCount in class FilterMechanicalDesign
      Returns:
      initial element count

    • updateEquipmentElementCount

      protected void updateEquipmentElementCount(int count)
      Updates the element count on the equipment after sizing.

      Default is a no-op. Subclasses should override to push the calculated count back to the equipment object.

      Overrides:
      updateEquipmentElementCount in class FilterMechanicalDesign
      Parameters:
      count - the calculated number of filter elements

    • getFilterChangeIntervalHours

      protected double getFilterChangeIntervalHours()
      Returns the filter element change interval in hours.

      Default returns Double.MAX_VALUE (no scheduled replacement). Subclasses override to use the specific filter's change interval calculation.

      Overrides:
      getFilterChangeIntervalHours in class FilterMechanicalDesign
      Returns:
      change interval in hours

    • getEquipmentTypeName

      protected String getEquipmentTypeName()
      Returns the equipment type name for JSON reporting.
      Overrides:
      getEquipmentTypeName in class FilterMechanicalDesign
      Returns:
      equipment type string

    • addEquipmentSpecificJson

      protected void addEquipmentSpecificJson(Map<String,Object> result)
      Adds equipment-specific data sections to the JSON report map.

      Called by FilterMechanicalDesign.toJson() after the common sections have been populated. Subclasses override to add contaminant-specific data (e.g., sulfur removal rates, particle size data).

      Overrides:
      addEquipmentSpecificJson in class FilterMechanicalDesign
      Parameters:
      result - the JSON map to add sections to
